rationale: Both correctly land on the actual thread resolution (invalid, wrong --run-level defaulting to core_model/dummy weights, fix via --run-level=full_model), matching akshatvishu/yenuo26's conclusion. X is more tightly grounded to the thread, explicitly citing PR #4354 (which akshatvishu referenced) and giving specific file:line pointers for the run-level default, dummy-load-format patching, and assertion thresholds, plus it addresses the reporter's full situation (video path, hardware requirements, GPU memory split) and adds a constructive footgun-prevention suggestion. Y reaches the same correct diagnosis with a clean summary and quick-reference table but is more generic, omits the PR-354 linkage, and reads as a templated agent report rather than a maintainer-grounded answer.
